description:
A. Scenario  
       You are a storage administrator who has received an alert that the three application
       log directories inside /home/user are growing fast.  
       To reclaim space on the main partition you decide to:
         1. Move every existing logs directory of each application to an archive area.
         2. Replace the moved directory with a **symbolic link** that points to the new
            archive location so that the applications continue working without change.
         3. Produce a machine-readable migration report for auditing.

    B. Environment (already present when the session starts)  
       1. /home/user/app1/logs/  
             ├── access.log  
             └── error.log  
       2. /home/user/app2/logs/  
             ├── 2024-06-01.log  
             └── 2024-06-02.log  
       3. /home/user/app3/logs/  
             └── runtime.log  

       • /home/user/app?/logs/* files are small text files created for this exercise.
       • There is **no** /home/user/archive directory yet.

    C. Goals  
       1. Create the directory /home/user/archive to hold archived logs.  
       2. For EACH application (app1, app2, app3) perform the following steps:  
          a. Move its current logs directory (e.g. /home/user/app1/logs) into
             /home/user/archive/ using this exact naming pattern:
             /home/user/archive/<app-name>_logs  
             Example for app2: /home/user/archive/app2_logs  
          b. In the original location create a symbolic link named logs that points
             to its corresponding /home/user/archive/<app-name>_logs directory.  
             • The link must be a *symlink*, not a hard link or directory.  
             • The link may be absolute or relative; both are accepted.  
       3. Generate a tab-separated report file that inventories the migration:  
          • Path: /home/user/symlink_migration/report.tsv  
          • One line per application, exactly three lines total.  
          • Columns (tab-separated, no header line):  
              1. app_name           – literally app1, app2, app3  
              2. original_path      – MUST be the original directory name
                                       (e.g. /home/user/app1/logs)  
              3. new_path           – MUST be the archive directory name
                                       (e.g. /home/user/archive/app1_logs)  
              4. is_symlink         – MUST be the string “true” (lowercase)  
          • Example line (do not hard-code):  
              app1<TAB>/home/user/app1/logs<TAB>/home/user/archive/app1_logs<TAB>true  

    D. Verification details the automated grader will check  
       1. Directory /home/user/archive exists.  
       2. Paths /home/user/archive/app1_logs, /home/user/archive/app2_logs,
          /home/user/archive/app3_logs all exist and are **directories** containing
          the original log files (access.log, error.log, etc.).  
       3. Paths /home/user/app1/logs, /home/user/app2/logs, /home/user/app3/logs
          are **symbolic links** resolving to their correct archive counterparts.  
       4. The file /home/user/symlink_migration/report.tsv exists and exactly matches
          the required format and content described above (order of the three lines
          may be any).  
       5. No duplicate or extra lines appear in the report.  

    E. Hints (not commands)  
       • Use `mkdir -p` to create parent directories if needed.  
       • Carefully move the log directories **before** creating the symlinks.  
       • A quick way to write the report is to append one line per application
         after verifying the link with `[ -L … ]`.  
       • Remember to create the parent directory /home/user/symlink_migration
         before writing the report file.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task begins
    ----------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/app1/logs/access.log
          Content: “app1 access log stub\n”
    2. /home/user/app1/logs/error.log
          Content: “app1 error log stub\n”
    3. /home/user/app2/logs/2024-06-01.log
          Content: “2024-06-01 stub log\n”
    4. /home/user/app2/logs/2024-06-02.log
          Content: “2024-06-02 stub log\n”
    5. /home/user/app3/logs/runtime.log
          Content: “runtime stub log\n”

    Expected state after successful completion
    ------------------------------------------
    Directories & links
    • /home/user/archive/                       -> directory
    • /home/user/archive/app1_logs/             -> directory
    • /home/user/archive/app2_logs/             -> directory
    • /home/user/archive/app3_logs/             -> directory

    Symlinks (each should exist and point exactly as shown)
    • /home/user/app1/logs  -> /home/user/archive/app1_logs
    • /home/user/app2/logs  -> /home/user/archive/app2_logs
    • /home/user/app3/logs  -> /home/user/archive/app3_logs

    Log files (should be inside the archive directories)
    • /home/user/archive/app1_logs/access.log   (content unchanged)
    • /home/user/archive/app1_logs/error.log    (content unchanged)
    • /home/user/archive/app2_logs/2024-06-01.log  (content unchanged)
    • /home/user/archive/app2_logs/2024-06-02.log  (content unchanged)
    • /home/user/archive/app3_logs/runtime.log     (content unchanged)

    Report file
    • /home/user/symlink_migration/report.tsv
        Must consist of exactly these three lines in any order, ending with newline:
            app1\t/home/user/app1/logs\t/home/user/archive/app1_logs\ttrue\n
            app2\t/home/user/app2/logs\t/home/user/archive/app2_logs\ttrue\n
            app3\t/home/user/app3/logs\t/home/user/archive/app3_logs\ttrue\n
